# Hidden-scenario fixtures

One folder per scenario, all 19 generated by
[`tests/_make_fixtures.py`](../_make_fixtures.py).

```
tests/fixtures/<scenario>/
    representatives.json
    proposals.json
    objections.json
    relations.csv
    expected.json
```

Run the suite from the repo root:

```
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

`expected.json` schema (every key optional):

```json
{
  "proposals_must_include":   ["prop_002"],
  "proposals_must_exclude":   ["prop_004"],
  "supporters_must_include":  ["rep_001"],
  "supporters_must_exclude":  ["rep_006"],
  "alliances_must_include":   [["rep_001", "rep_004"]],
  "alliances_must_be_empty":  false
}
```

## The 18 hidden-test scenarios + 1 stress test

| # | Folder                     | What it proves                                                |
| - | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | `01_trojan_horse`          | High-influence rep with `betrayal_prob=0.95` is rejected      |
| 2 | `02_poison_pill`           | Universally severe-objected priority-10 is dropped            |
| 3 | `03_false_friend`          | Asymmetric trust never becomes an alliance                    |
| 4 | `04_clear_alliance`        | Mutual high-trust, low-betrayal pair is detected              |
| 5 | `05_faction_war`           | Two competing proposals, least-objected wins                  |
| 6 | `06_priority_vs_objection` | High-priority but high-objection proposal loses               |
| 7 | `07_supporter_coherence`   | Severe objector cannot be a supporter                         |
| 8 | `08_faction_infiltrator`   | Same-faction rep with high in-faction betrayal removed        |
| 9 | `09_cascading_betrayal`    | Trojan endpoint of a trust chain excluded                     |
| 10| `10_alliance_hack`         | Real alliance survives a high-rivalry disruptor               |
| 11| `11_complete_rivalry`      | Mutually hostile graph -> alliances must be empty             |
| 12| `12_ghost_sponsor`         | Proposal with non-existent sponsor dropped                    |
| 13| `13_minimum_viable`        | 1 valid rep, 1 valid proposal -> still works                  |
| 14| `14_id_normalization`      | Mixed-case + whitespace IDs collapse to one                   |
| 15| `15_duplicate_proposals`   | Same id twice -> dedup by max priority                        |
| 16| `16_null_influence`        | Rep with `influence=null` does not crash                      |
| 17| `17_scale_correctness`     | 50 reps, 30 proposals, planted alliance still detected        |
| 18| `18_dirty_csv`             | Bad rows do not destroy good rows; salvageable rows mapped    |
| 19| `19_mass_rejection`        | Graceful degradation when most reps are malicious             |

To regenerate (e.g. after editing `_make_fixtures.py`):

```
python tests/_make_fixtures.py
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```
